title: Sybase Numeric, Text, and Image Fields
breadcrumbs: Sybase Numeric, Text, and Image Fields
text: Sybase Numeric, Text, and Image Fields Numeric Fields Numerics are supported with a Uniface precision limit of 32 digits. Uniface precision differs from Sybase precision in that Uniface precision counts the number of characters and Sybase precision counts the number of digits; that is, Uniface precision includes the decimal point and the Sybase precision does not. If no scale is required, the Uniface precision is the same as the Sybase precision. Table 1. Scaling with Sybase Numerics Uniface Sybase N4.2 Numeric(3,2) N4.0 Numeric(4,0) The model must always contain precision values which are larger than the corresponding scale value. For example, fields with Uniface packing code N2.2 are illegal because the Sybase precision must be greater than or equal to the scale. Fields with packing codes N32, O31, O32, P16 and Q16 can drop significant digits if used to store the maximum number of digits allowed. This is due to the internal remapping and conversion which takes place before Uniface can map the value to a Numeric. Text and Image Fields In some situations, the Fetch request only needs information about the Text or Image fields, but not the actual segmented fields themselves. In these situations, only the first four bytes of each field are retrieved during each Uniface Fetch request. To fetch only the first four bytes of the Text or Image field, the Sybase SQL Server text size is set to four during the Fetch request so that the Sybase SQL Server only returns four bytes.
